with:
  def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
      """
      Convert a list of triples about an artist into a fluent natural‑language description.
      """
      if not triples:
          return ""
  
      # Group triples by subject to avoid repeating the subject name
      subj_map: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
      for t in triples:
          subj_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)
  
      # Helper functions for phrase generation (without repeating subject)
      def phrase_for(pred: str, obj: str) -> str:
          # Normalise object (remove surrounding quotes if present)
          if isinstance(obj, str) and obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"'):
              obj = obj[1:-1]
          # Specific phrasing
          if pred == "birthDate":
              return f"was born on {obj}"
          if pred == "birthPlace":
              return f"was born in {obj}"
          if pred == "origin":
              return f"originates from {obj}"
          if pred == "genre":
              return f"performs {obj} music"
          if pred == "instrument":
              return f"plays the {obj}"
          if pred == "occupation":
              return f"works as {obj}"
  ... (67 more lines)
